steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The model prescribes a single uniform methane mole fraction because observational data are too sparse and discrepant to determine Titan's methane levels precisely. We need the choice that follows logically from this deliberate simplification.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the model will overestimate methane': The passage gives no basis for predicting the direction of any error — only that uncertainty exists. ✗
  • Choice B — 'inconsistencies within the model': The passage is concerned with how the model compares to real observations, not with internal model consistency. ✗
  • Choice C — 'better observations would make the model unnecessary': Better data would actually improve the model, not eliminate the need for it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'disagreements with actual observations are to be expected': A model that reduces uncertain, variable reality to a single fixed value will necessarily diverge from some real measurements — those disagreements are an expected consequence of the simplification. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
